title: Word Ladder
problem_statement: Return the length of the shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord using one-letter intermediate words from a dictionary.

examples.explanation: One shortest sequence is hit -> hot -> dot -> dog -> cog.

constraints: All words contain only lowercase letters.
intuition: Use BFS on the word graph where edges connect words that differ by one letter, and stop when endWord is reached.

approaches.name: Single-direction BFS
approaches.description: Generate neighbors by changing one letter at a time and search breadth-first for the target word.
approaches.why_not: DFS may find longer paths and is inefficient for shortest sequence search.

interview_qa.q: Why remove words from the dictionary once used?
interview_qa.a: To avoid revisiting and keep BFS efficient.

interview_qa.q: What does each BFS layer represent?
